claims.summary: The live six-item continuity window risks turning older high-meaning scars into texture without history.
claims.rationale: The live six-item lane remains bounded, but quiet scar/warmth/low-frequency pressure/plateau terms now qualify for existing decayed afterimages with a focused regression.

claims.summary: Increasing the live trajectory limit to 10 would be a prompt/report behavior change with possible context-pressure effects.
claims.rationale: Implemented a more precise afterimage classifier instead of raising the live trajectory limit, preserving bounded delivery while acting on the felt report.

claims.summary: Shadow-v3 and Minime volatility comparisons need runtime or sandbox observation before any live authority change.
claims.rationale: No live pressure, Minime mode-packing, peer mutation, or controller change was made; existing sandbox queue infrastructure remains the right path for future comparative probes.
